name: ad_video_generation
objective: Turn product intelligence, selected angle, script, storyboard, and production package into a connected step-by-step video creation workflow.

source_artifacts.label: Project inputs
source_artifacts.description: Product name, category, short description, audience, goal, platform, duration, tone, CTA, claims to avoid, and uploaded files.

source_artifacts.label: Product intelligence
source_artifacts.description: The compact product brief used to decide proof points, audience, product role, safe claims, and visual direction.

source_artifacts.label: Selected creative angle
source_artifacts.description: The hook, pain point, emotional trigger, product role, proof moment, CTA, and score selected for this variant.

source_artifacts.label: Variant script
source_artifacts.description: The final ad script, voiceover, title, caption, cover prompt, and storyboard generated for this variant.

source_artifacts.label: Production package
source_artifacts.description: Character bible, character reference prompts, production scenes, UI overlay plan, edit plan, and render sequence.

stages.label: Character lock
stages.purpose: Create a stable actor identity before generating scenes so later keyframes and clips look like the same person.
stages.manual_goal: Generate or choose reference images for the actor, then upload the approved files back to the workflow.
review_focus: The same actor identity is preserved across all reference images.
Only pose, framing, or expression changes between references.
The actor can plausibly perform the selected ad angle.
success_criteria: A clean reference image exists for each required reference id.
Face, age, hair, wardrobe, and overall identity stay consistent.
Reference images are suitable as inputs for keyframe and video generation.

stages.label: Scene keyframes
stages.purpose: Create one controllable still image per production scene before video generation.
stages.manual_goal: Upload reference images into an image tool, paste the keyframe prompt, and return one approved keyframe per scene.
review_focus: The scene advances the chosen hook and proof moment.
The character still matches the reference identity.
Product/app UI areas are clean enough for readable overlays later.
success_criteria: One 9:16 keyframe exists for each scene.
Each keyframe matches its storyboard beat and visual objective.
No generated fake app text conflicts with the later UI overlay plan.

stages.label: Image-to-video clips
stages.purpose: Animate each approved keyframe into a short scene clip while preserving identity, product context, and timing.
stages.manual_goal: Upload each keyframe to a video tool such as Kling image-to-video, paste the video prompt and motion notes, then upload the generated clip.
review_focus: Identity remains stable across the clip.
Motion follows the action description and does not invent extra story beats.
The clip duration and aspect ratio match the scene settings.
success_criteria: One raw video clip exists for each scene.
The clip has no major face morphing, product hallucination, or unusable camera movement.
Clip output can be edited in the final assembly stage.

stages.label: Product and UI overlays
stages.purpose: Add real app screenshots, subtitles, disclaimers, CTA, or logo as editor overlays after video generation.
stages.manual_goal: Use a video editor to place uploaded app/product assets and safety copy on top of generated clips.
review_focus: App UI and disclaimers are readable on mobile.
Overlay timing matches voiceover and scene action.
Safe claims from Product Intelligence are respected.
success_criteria: Every overlay scene has a finished edited clip.
No required disclaimer, CTA, or app UI moment is missing.
Overlays do not hide important action or faces.

stages.label: Edit assembly
stages.purpose: Cut the completed scene clips into one ad with voiceover, subtitles, music, CTA, and pacing.
stages.manual_goal: Assemble clips in timeline order, add audio/subtitles/music, and upload the master video.
review_focus: The final timeline follows hook -> demo/proof -> payoff -> CTA.
Voiceover, subtitle, and scene timing stay aligned.
The ad fits the requested duration and platform.
success_criteria: No timeline gaps, missing scenes, unreadable subtitles, or broken audio.
The master is ready for platform-specific exports.

stages.label: Platform exports
stages.purpose: Create placement-ready versions such as 9:16 and 1:1 from the approved master.
stages.manual_goal: Resize, crop, and check readable safe zones for every final placement.
review_focus: Captions, CTA, app UI, and logo remain in safe zones.
Export specs match the selected platform.
The output file can be uploaded to ads platforms.
success_criteria: Each export is playable and readable on mobile.
The exported filenames and aspect ratios are clear.

provider_requirements.notes: Used for character reference images and scene keyframes.
Used for product UI overlays and compliance copy.
Used to cut all scenes into the final ad.
